topNews.title: Uber's $1,500/Month AI Limit Is a Useful Signal for AI Tool Pricing
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Uber's $1,500/Month AI Limit Is a Useful Signal for AI Tool Pricing
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Uber set a $1,500 monthly limit on AI tool spending.  
• Why it matters: This cap signals potential pricing strategies for AI tools across the industry.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this insight to optimize their AI tool budgets and pricing models.

topNews.title: These two founders left Goldman and Meta to build voice AI for markets everyone else overlooked
topNews.rawTextSnippet: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Founders from Goldman and Meta launched a voice AI startup focusing on underserved markets.  
• Why it matters: The startup is processing over 17,000 calls daily in Africa and the Middle East, highlighting significant demand.  
• Business opportunity: There is potential for growth in voice AI solutions tailored for emerging markets.

topNews.title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
topNews.rawTextSnippet: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the U.K. then rolled out globally.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: U.K. regulators mandate Google to provide a tool for publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features.  
• Why it matters: This regulation empowers publishers to control their content's use in AI, potentially affecting their revenue models.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can develop solutions to help publishers manage their online presence and optimize content for AI search.

topNews.title: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
topNews.rawTextSnippet: The Series F round values Coralogix at $1.6 billion and comes less than a year after its previous raise.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Coralogix secured $200M in a Series F funding round, valuing the company at $1.6 billion.  
• Why it matters: This investment highlights the growing demand for monitoring AI agents in business operations.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage Coralogix's solutions to enhance AI oversight and improve operational efficiency.

topNews.title: Expanding Project Glasswing
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Anthropic announced the expansion of Project Glasswing, enhancing its AI capabilities.  
• Why it matters: This expansion signifies a competitive advancement in AI technology, potentially reshaping industry standards.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage these advanced AI features for improved operational efficiency and innovative product development.

topNews.title: The Download: Trump’s new AI order, and smart glasses for warfare
topNews.rawTextSnippet: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. 5 key points in Trump’s new AI order Less than two weeks after scrapping an executive order on AI, President Donald Trump signed a new one on Tu…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: President Trump signed a new executive order on AI shortly after rescinding a previous one.  
• Why it matters: This move signals a renewed focus on AI policy and regulation amid growing global competition.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can capitalize on emerging AI technologies and government contracts related to defense and innovation.

topNews.title: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
topNews.rawTextSnippet: The specification lets developer, compliance, and security teams define their own policies for agents to follow in portable policy files.
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Microsoft introduced a specification allowing developers to set custom policies for AI agents.  
• Why it matters: This enhances control over AI behavior, improving compliance and security for businesses.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage this feature to create tailored AI solutions that meet specific regulatory requirements.

topNews.title: A weekend benchmarking Copilot CLI's /security-review across 5 LLMs
topNews.rawTextSnippet: A weekend benchmarking Copilot CLI's /security-review across 5 LLMs
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Benchmarking of Copilot CLI's /security-review was conducted across five large language models (LLMs).  
• Why it matters: This evaluation helps identify the strengths and weaknesses of different LLMs in security contexts.  
• Business opportunity: Companies can leverage insights from this benchmarking to enhance their own LLM security features and offerings.

topNews.title: Show HN: Nano – open core siem built on rust and ClickHouse
topNews.rawTextSnippet: Hi HN, I’m Dan Lussier & I built a SIEM named nano. The platform took around 6 months to be fully featured (and tested, and security scanned.. many times over). I’ve been working in information security for over 20 years, and even if it sounds redundant, I always found major SIEM providers to 1,…
topNews.summary:
• What happened: Dan Lussier launched Nano, an open-core SIEM built on Rust and ClickHouse.  
• Why it matters: Nano addresses gaps in existing SIEM solutions, offering enhanced security and performance.  
• Business opportunity: Companies seeking efficient and customizable security solutions may adopt Nano to improve their cybersecurity infrastructure.

funding.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos  to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries
funding.rawTextSnippet: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries — targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications where a cyberattack could affect 100 million people.

funding.title: Anthropic faces AI spending backlash before IPO
funding.rawTextSnippet: Anthropic faces AI spending backlash before IPO

dailyBrief: In today's AI landscape, Uber's decision to cap its AI tool pricing at $1,500 per month signals a potential shift in how companies might approach budgeting for AI technologies, emphasizing the need for cost-effective solutions. Meanwhile, two former Goldman Sachs and Meta founders are venturing into the overlooked voice AI market, highlighting opportunities for innovation in niche sectors. On the regulatory front, new rules allowing publishers to opt out of AI Search could reshape content monetization strategies, impacting how businesses engage with AI-driven platforms. Additionally, Coralogix's recent $200 million funding round underscores the growing demand for monitoring AI agents, indicating a robust market for AI oversight solutions. Lastly, the expansion of Project Glasswing suggests a continued commitment to advancing AI capabilities, potentially influencing various industries.
